meta.title: الكود المصري للحماية من الحرائق
meta.publisher: Egyptian Organization for Standardization and Quality (EOS)
meta.scope: Fire protection requirements for all building types in Egypt
meta.notes: Egyptian code draws heavily from NFPA standards with local adaptations. NFPA 72 is the primary referenced standard for fire alarm systems in Egypt.
meta.jurisdiction: Arab Republic of Egypt — mandatory for all new construction and major renovations

fire_alarm_mandatory.notes: Local AHJ (Civil Defense / الدفاع المدني) has final authority

referenced_standards.primary: NFPA 72 (National Fire Alarm and Signaling Code)
secondary: NFPA 101 (Life Safety Code) for occupancy requirements
NFPA 170 (Fire Safety Symbols) for drawing symbols
local_codes: Egyptian Building Code (الكود المصري للتصميم الإنشائي)
Egyptian Electrical Code (based on IEC standards)

approval_process: Submit fire alarm shop drawings to Civil Defense for review
Obtain Civil Defense approval before installation
Acceptance testing witnessed by Civil Defense inspector
System certificate valid for 1 year (must be renewed)
documentation_required: Approved shop drawings ( stamped by licensed fire protection engineer)
Manufacturer certificates for all equipment (EN 54 / UL listed)

contractor_requirements.license: Must hold valid Civil Defense license for fire alarm installation
contractor_requirements.engineer: Design must be stamped by licensed fire protection engineer
contractor_requirements.technicians: Installation and maintenance must be performed by certified technicians

differences_from_nfpa.egypt: Voice alarm messages must be in Arabic (bilingual Arabic/English acceptable)
All fire alarm signage must be bilingual (Arabic + English)
Direct connection to Civil Defense monitoring center required in major cities
24VDC system standard (panels have universal power supplies)
Annual Civil Defense inspection mandatory; maintenance contract required

specific_egyptian_requirements.notes: Always verify current requirements with local Civil Defense office — requirements may vary by governorate

occupancy_classification_egypt.description: Egyptian building code classifies buildings for fire protection purposes

categories.examples: Theaters, cinemas, mosques, churches, conference halls
Chemical storage, fuel stations, gas facilities

occupancy_classification_egypt.notes: Classification determines the fire alarm category, sprinkler requirements, and other fire protection systems
